title: What the Bible says about self-control (it's not white-knuckling)
caption:
You're not undisciplined. You're ungoverned.

Proverbs 25:28 calls a man without self-control 'a city broken into and left without walls.' That's not a moral lecture — that's a vulnerability assessment. The enemy doesn't need to fight a fortified man. He just walks through the open gate.

And here's what most men get wrong: Scripture doesn't say build discipline. Galatians 5:23 lists self-control as a fruit of the Spirit — it grows as you grow in Him. You can't white-knuckle your way to what only the Spirit produces.

Start with the three gates: the phone in your hand, the alarm you don't obey, the door you keep reopening. Govern those and everything downstream shifts.

The governed man gets trusted with more. That's not theory — it's a pattern.

Save this for the man who's trying harder instead of going deeper.

#faith #christianmen #discipline #selfcontrol #godlymen #manofgod #scripture #proverbs
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
